title: Subsets
questionBody:
Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).

The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.

 

Example 1:

Input: nums = [1,2,3]
Output: [[],[1],[2],[1,2],[3],[1,3],[2,3],[1,2,3]]

Example 2:

Input: nums = [0]
Output: [[],[0]]

 

Constraints:

  • 1 <= nums.length <= 10
  • -10 <= nums[i] <= 10
  • All the numbers of nums are unique.
plainEnglishBody:
Given an array of unique integers called nums,

return all possible subsets (the power set).

The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the list of subsets in any order.

examples.explanation: An empty array has only one subset, which is the empty set itself.
The subsets of [0] are the empty set and the set containing 0.
The subsets are the empty set, {1}, {2}, and {1, 2}.
We include all combinations of the numbers 1, 2, and 3, including the empty set.
All possible combinations including the empty set form the power set.

clarifyingQuestions: What is the maximum size of the input array `nums`?
Can the input array `nums` contain negative numbers?
Is the order of subsets in the output important, or can they be in any order?
Is the order of elements within each subset important, or can they be in any order?
Is the input array `nums` guaranteed to be non-null?

edgeCases.handling: Return a list containing only an empty list to represent the empty subset.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Input array with a single element
edgeCases.handling: Return a list containing an empty list and a list containing the single element.
Ensure the algorithm's time complexity is acceptable, given that the number of subsets grows exponentially (O(2^n)).
The standard subset generation algorithms should handle negative numbers without any special modifications.
The standard subset generation algorithms should handle zero without any special modifications.
Consider the maximum size of the input array and whether the bit representation of subsets will exceed the integer limits and switch to a different subset generation algorithm if needed.

edgeCases.edgeCase: Inputs close to maximum integer values
edgeCases.handling: Standard subset generation will handle extreme boundary values as they are, as long as we're not performing arithmetic operations prone to overflows.

edgeCases.edgeCase: All numbers are identical (though the problem statement specifies 'unique integers')
edgeCases.handling: Despite being unlikely due to constraints, standard subset generation will work since we only care about if an element is present or not and not about distinct elements.

bruteForce.overview: The goal is to find all possible combinations of items from a given set. A brute force approach means we're going to explore every single possible combination. Think of it as exhaustively trying everything.
steps: Start with an empty group. This is always a possibility: choosing nothing.
Then, consider each item individually. Each item, by itself, is a possible group.
Next, consider every pair of items. Take each pair and see if it forms a valid group.
After pairs, look at every possible group of three items. Then groups of four, and so on.
Keep going until you've considered a group that includes all the items. This is also a valid group.
By the end, you will have tried every possible combination of items, giving you all the subsets.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: The algorithm systematically generates subsets of all possible sizes from the empty set to the set containing all n elements.  Generating subsets of size k from a set of n elements takes n choose k operations. Summing this for all possible subset sizes (from k=0 to k=n) results in the sum of binomial coefficients, which is equal to 2^n.  Therefore, the time complexity is O(2^n) because we are essentially exploring every possible combination of elements to form subsets.
bigO.spaceExplanation: The brute force approach described involves constructing all possible subsets. Each subset is stored as a separate group. In the worst case, where N is the number of items in the input set, we could potentially store a group containing all N items. While the algorithm generates 2^N subsets, only one subset of size N may be stored at any given time, such as when considering the subset with all items. Therefore, the space complexity is determined by the largest subset being constructed, which can contain up to N items, resulting in O(N) auxiliary space.

Python.html:
def find_all_subsets_brute_force(input_set):
    all_subsets = []

    number_of_elements = len(input_set)

    # Start with an empty set, which is always a subset
    all_subsets.append([])

    for subset_size in range(1, number_of_elements + 1):
        for i in range(1 << number_of_elements):
            # Generate all possible combinations of given size
            if bin(i).count('1') == subset_size:
                current_subset = []

                for j in range(number_of_elements):
                    # Check if the j-th bit is set in the current combination
                    if (i >> j) & 1:
                        current_subset.append(input_set[j])

                all_subsets.append(current_subset)

    return all_subsets

JavaScript.html:
function findSubsetsBruteForce(numbers) {
  const allSubsets = [];
  const numberOfElements = numbers.length;

  // Add the empty set as the first subset
  allSubsets.push([]);

  // Iterate through all possible subset sizes
  for (let subsetSize = 1; subsetSize <= numberOfElements; subsetSize++) {
    // Iterate through all possible starting indices for the subset
    for (let i = 0; i < (1 << numberOfElements); i++) {
      if (countSetBits(i) === subsetSize) {
        const currentSubset = [];

        // Build the subset based on the bits that are set in the current index
        for (let j = 0; j < numberOfElements; j++) {
          if ((i >> j) & 1) {
            currentSubset.push(numbers[j]);
          }
        }
        allSubsets.push(currentSubset);
      }
    }
  }

  return allSubsets;

  function countSetBits(number) {
    let count = 0;
    while (number > 0) {
      number &= (number - 1);
      count++;
    }
    return count;
  }
}

TypeScript.html:
function generateAllSubsetsBruteForce(originalSet: number[]): number[][] {
    const allSubsets: number[][] = [];
    const numberOfElements = originalSet.length;

    // Add the empty set as the first subset.
    allSubsets.push([]);

    // Iterate through all possible subset sizes.
    for (let subsetSize = 1; subsetSize <= numberOfElements; subsetSize++) {
      // Generate all combinations of the current subset size.
      generateCombinations(originalSet, subsetSize, 0, [], allSubsets);
    }

    return allSubsets;

    function generateCombinations(
      originalSet: number[],
      subsetSize: number,
      startIndex: number,
      currentCombination: number[],
      allSubsets: number[][]
    ): void {
      if (currentCombination.length === subsetSize) {
        // Base case: a subset of the desired size has been created.
        // Add a copy to the master list so it isn't mutated
        allSubsets.push([...currentCombination]);
        return;
      }

      // Iterate through all remaining elements in the original set.
      for (let index = startIndex; index < originalSet.length; index++) {
        // Include the current element in the combination.
        currentCombination.push(originalSet[index]);

        // Recursively generate combinations including this element.
        generateCombinations(
          originalSet,
          subsetSize,
          index + 1,
          currentCombination,
          allSubsets
        );

        // Backtrack to remove the last element and try the next one.
        currentCombination.pop();
      }
    }
  }

Java.html:
public class Solution {

    public List<List<Integer>> subsets(int[] numbers) {
        List<List<Integer>> allSubsets = new ArrayList<>();
        int numberOfElements = numbers.length;

        // Start with an empty set. Always a possibility.
        allSubsets.add(new ArrayList<>());

        for (int subsetSize = 1; subsetSize <= numberOfElements; subsetSize++) {
            // Iterate over all possible subset sizes.
            generateSubsets(numbers, subsetSize, 0, new ArrayList<>(), allSubsets);
        }

        return allSubsets;
    }

    private void generateSubsets(int[] numbers, int subsetSize, int startIndex, 
                                 List<Integer> currentSubset, List<List<Integer>> allSubsets) {

        if (currentSubset.size() == subsetSize) {
            // Add a copy of the current subset to the result
            allSubsets.add(new ArrayList<>(currentSubset));

            return;
        }

        // Ensure we don't go out of bounds
        for (int i = startIndex; i < numbers.length; i++) {
            // Explore including the current number in the subset
            currentSubset.add(numbers[i]);

            // Recursively generate subsets with the updated subset
            generateSubsets(numbers, subsetSize, i + 1, currentSubset, allSubsets);

            // Backtrack: Remove the last element to explore other branches
            currentSubset.remove(currentSubset.size() - 1);
        }
    }
}

C++.html:
#include <vector>

std::vector<std::vector<int>> subsets(std::vector<int>& nums) {
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> allSubsets;
    int numElements = nums.size();

    // Start with the empty set
    allSubsets.push_back({});

    // Generate subsets of length 1 to numElements
    for (int subsetSize = 1; subsetSize <= numElements; ++subsetSize) {
        for (int i = 0; i < (1 << numElements); ++i) {
            if (__builtin_popcount(i) == subsetSize) {

                // We only care about combinations of the current subsetSize
                std::vector<int> currentSubset;

                for (int j = 0; j < numElements; ++j) {
                    if ((i >> j) & 1) {
                        currentSubset.push_back(nums[j]);
                    }
                }

                allSubsets.push_back(currentSubset);
            }
        }
    }

    return allSubsets;
}

C#.html:
public class Solution {

    public IList<IList<int>> Subsets(int[] numbers) {
        IList<IList<int>> allSubsets = new List<IList<int>>();
        int numbersLength = numbers.Length;

        // Start with an empty set
        allSubsets.Add(new List<int>());

        // Generate subsets of length 1 to numbersLength
        for (int subsetSize = 1; subsetSize <= numbersLength; subsetSize++)
        {
            GenerateSubsets(numbers, subsetSize, 0, new List<int>(), allSubsets);
        }

        return allSubsets;
    }

    private void GenerateSubsets(int[] numbers, int subsetSize, int startIndex, List<int> currentSubset, IList<IList<int>> allSubsets)
    {
        // If we have reached the desired subset size, add the subset
        if (currentSubset.Count == subsetSize)
        {
            allSubsets.Add(new List<int>(currentSubset));
            return;
        }

        // Iterate through the remaining elements to form subsets
        for (int i = startIndex; i < numbers.Length; i++)
        {
            // Include the current element in the subset
            currentSubset.Add(numbers[i]);
            
            // Recursively generate subsets with the next index
            // Allows to select distinct combinations
            GenerateSubsets(numbers, subsetSize, i + 1, currentSubset, allSubsets);

            // Backtrack: Remove the current element to explore
            // other possible subsets
            currentSubset.RemoveAt(currentSubset.Count - 1);
        }
    }
}

Go.html:
package main

func subsetsBruteForce(numbers []int) [][]int {
	subsets := [][]int{{}}

	for subsetSize := 1; subsetSize <= len(numbers); subsetSize++ {
		// Iterate through all possible subset sizes
		combinations := generateCombinations(numbers, subsetSize)
		subsets = append(subsets, combinations...)
	}

	return subsets
}

func generateCombinations(numbers []int, subsetSize int) [][]int {
	combinations := [][]int{}
	currentCombination := []int{}

	generateCombinationsRecursive(numbers, subsetSize, 0, currentCombination, &combinations)

	return combinations
}

func generateCombinationsRecursive(numbers []int, subsetSize int, startIndex int, currentCombination []int, combinations *[][]int) {
	// Base case: If the current combination is the desired size, add it to the result
	if len(currentCombination) == subsetSize {
		combinationCopy := make([]int, len(currentCombination))
		copy(combinationCopy, currentCombination)
		*combinations = append(*combinations, combinationCopy)
		return
	}

	// Optimization to avoid index out of range errors
	if startIndex >= len(numbers) {
		return
	}

	// Include the current number in the combination
	currentCombination = append(currentCombination, numbers[startIndex])
	generateCombinationsRecursive(numbers, subsetSize, startIndex+1, currentCombination, combinations)

	// Exclude the current number from the combination
	currentCombination = currentCombination[:len(currentCombination)-1]
	// Skip current number and find the next one
	generateCombinationsRecursive(numbers, subsetSize, startIndex+1, currentCombination, combinations)

}

Rust.html:
fn subsets_brute_force(numbers: Vec<i32>) -> Vec<Vec<i32>> {
    let mut all_subsets: Vec<Vec<i32>> = Vec::new();

    // Start with the empty set, which is always a subset.
    all_subsets.push(Vec::new());

    let numbers_length = numbers.len();

    // Iterate through all possible subset sizes.
    for subset_size in 1..=numbers_length {
      
      // Generate all combinations of the given size.
      let mut combination_indices: Vec<usize> = (0..subset_size).collect();

      loop {
        let mut current_subset: Vec<i32> = Vec::new();

        // Create subset based on current combination of indices.
        for &index in &combination_indices {
          current_subset.push(numbers[index]);
        }
        all_subsets.push(current_subset);

        // Find the rightmost index to increment.
        let mut index_to_increment = subset_size as i32 - 1;

        // Move left until we find an index that can be incremented.
        while index_to_increment >= 0 && combination_indices[index_to_increment as usize] == numbers_length - subset_size + index_to_increment as usize {
          index_to_increment -= 1;
        }

        //If no index to increment, current size subsets generated.
        if index_to_increment < 0 {
          break;
        }

        // Increment the index and update the subsequent indices.
        combination_indices[index_to_increment as usize] += 1;

        // The rest of the numbers combine with previous ones.
        for j in (index_to_increment + 1) as usize..subset_size {
          combination_indices[j] = combination_indices[j - 1] + 1;
        }
      }
    }

    all_subsets
  }

Kotlin.html:
fun subsetsBruteForce(numbers: IntArray): List<List<Int>> {
    val allSubsets: MutableList<MutableList<Int>> = mutableListOf()

    // Add the empty set first because it is always a subset
    allSubsets.add(mutableListOf())

    // Iterate through all possible sizes of subsets
    for (subsetSize in 1..numbers.size) {
      // Iterate through all possible starting positions for subsets of the current size
      for (i in 0..numbers.size - subsetSize) {
        // Create a subset of the current size, starting at index i
        val currentSubset: MutableList<Int> = mutableListOf()

        // Populate currentSubset
        for (j in 0 until subsetSize) {
          currentSubset.add(numbers[i + j])
        }

        allSubsets.add(currentSubset)
      }
    }

    // Need to account for subsets that are not contiguous
    for (i in 0 until (1 shl numbers.size)) {
      val currentSubset: MutableList<Int> = mutableListOf()

      // Construct each subset based on the bits of i
      for (j in 0 until numbers.size) {
        //Check each bit to see if it is set
        if (i and (1 shl j) != 0) {
          currentSubset.add(numbers[j])
        }
      }
      if(currentSubset.isNotEmpty()){
        allSubsets.add(currentSubset)
      }
    }
    // This de-duplicates to ensure only unique subsets are returned
    return allSubsets.distinct().toList()
  }

Swift.html:
func findSubsetsBruteForce(numbers: [Int]) -> [[Int]] {
    var allSubsets: [[Int]] = []
    
    // Add the empty set to start, 
s    // as choosing nothing is always an option.
    allSubsets.append([])

    for subsetSize in 1...numbers.count {
        
        // Generate all possible subsets for this size.
        generateSubsets(currentSubset: [], numbers: numbers, start: 0, subsetSize: subsetSize, allSubsets: &allSubsets)
    }

    return allSubsets
}

func generateSubsets(currentSubset: [Int], numbers: [Int], start: Int, subsetSize: Int, allSubsets: inout [[Int]]) {
    if currentSubset.count == subsetSize {
        allSubsets.append(currentSubset)
        return
    }
    
    // Iterate through numbers to find elements
    // to add to subsets.
    for index in start..<numbers.count {
        var nextSubset = currentSubset

        // Add current number to subset.
        nextSubset.append(numbers[index])
        
        // Recursively call until subset size is met.
        generateSubsets(currentSubset: nextSubset, numbers: numbers, start: index + 1, subsetSize: subsetSize, allSubsets: &allSubsets)
    }
}

Ruby.code:
def find_all_subsets(numbers)
  all_subsets = [[]]

  numbers.each do |number|
    new_subsets = []

    # Iterate through existing subsets to generate new ones.
    all_subsets.each do |existing_subset|

      # Create a new subset by including the current number.
      new_subset = existing_subset + [number]
      new_subsets << new_subset
    end

    # Add the new subsets to the list.
    # This builds the subsets with the current element.
    all_subsets.concat(new_subsets)
  end

  return all_subsets
end

def find_all_subsets(numbers)
  all_subsets = [[]]

  numbers.each do |number|
    new_subsets = []

    # Iterate through existing subsets to generate new ones.
    all_subsets.each do |existing_subset|

      # Create a new subset by including the current number.
      new_subset = existing_subset + [number]
      new_subsets << new_subset
    end

    # Add the new subsets to the list.
    # This builds the subsets with the current element.
    all_subsets.concat(new_subsets)
  end

  return all_subsets
end

PHP.code:
<?php
function findSubsetsBruteForce(array $inputNumbers): array
{
    $allSubsets = [];

    // Add the empty set as a subset
    $allSubsets[] = [];

    $inputSize = count($inputNumbers);

    // Iterate through all possible subset sizes
    for ($subsetSize = 1; $subsetSize <= $inputSize; $subsetSize++) {
        // Generate all combinations of the given size
        $combinations = generateCombinations($inputNumbers, $subsetSize);

        // Add the combinations to the result
        foreach ($combinations as $combination) {
            $allSubsets[] = $combination;
        }
    }

    return $allSubsets;
}

function generateCombinations(array $inputNumbers, int $subsetSize, int $startIndex = 0, array $currentCombination = []): array
{
    $combinations = [];

    // If the current combination is the desired size, add it to the result
    if (count($currentCombination) === $subsetSize) {
        $combinations[] = $currentCombination;
        return $combinations;
    }

    // If there are not enough elements left to form a subset of the required size, return
    if (count($inputNumbers) - $startIndex < $subsetSize - count($currentCombination)) {
        return [];
    }

    // Iterate through the remaining elements in the array
    for ($index = $startIndex; $index < count($inputNumbers); $index++) {
        // Include the current element in the combination
        $newCombination = array_merge($currentCombination, [$inputNumbers[$index]]);

        // Recursively generate combinations with the current element included
        $newCombinations = generateCombinations($inputNumbers, $subsetSize, $index + 1, $newCombination);

        // Add the new combinations to the result
        $combinations = array_merge($combinations, $newCombinations);
    }

    return $combinations;
}

?>

function generateCombinations(array $inputNumbers, int $subsetSize, int $startIndex = 0, array $currentCombination = []): array
{
    $combinations = [];

    // If the current combination is the desired size, add it to the result
    if (count($currentCombination) === $subsetSize) {
        $combinations[] = $currentCombination;
        return $combinations;
    }

    // If there are not enough elements left to form a subset of the required size, return
    if (count($inputNumbers) - $startIndex < $subsetSize - count($currentCombination)) {
        return [];
    }

    // Iterate through the remaining elements in the array
    for ($index = $startIndex; $index < count($inputNumbers); $index++) {
        // Include the current element in the combination
        $newCombination = array_merge($currentCombination, [$inputNumbers[$index]]);

        // Recursively generate combinations with the current element included
        $newCombinations = generateCombinations($inputNumbers, $subsetSize, $index + 1, $newCombination);

        // Add the new combinations to the result
        $combinations = array_merge($combinations, $newCombinations);
    }

    return $combinations;
}

optimal.overview: The best way to find all subsets is to think about making a decision for each item: do we include it in a subset, or not? We build up all possible subsets by systematically going through each item and considering both possibilities.
steps: Consider the first item. We now have two choices: either include it in our current subsets, or don't.
If we include it, we add a copy of all the current subsets with the item added to each one.
If we don't include it, we keep the current subsets as they are.
Now we have a new collection of subsets, representing all choices made so far.
Repeat this process for each remaining item in the original set.
At each step, we're doubling the number of subsets as we decide whether or not to include the current item.
Once we've gone through all the items, the final collection of subsets represents all possible combinations, including the empty set and the set itself.

bigO.runtimeExplanation: The algorithm iterates through each of the n elements in the input set. For each element, it effectively doubles the number of subsets accumulated so far. Since there are 2^n possible subsets in total, constructing each subset takes O(n) time (to copy the existing subset and potentially add the current element). Therefore, the overall time complexity is O(n * 2^n).
bigO.spaceExplanation: The algorithm generates all possible subsets of the input set. At each step, we are doubling the number of subsets we store. This means we are creating a new collection of subsets, where each subset potentially has a size of up to N (the number of elements in the input set). Therefore, the space required to store all 2^N subsets is proportional to N * 2^N in the worst case since each subset can have at most N elements. However, when considering space complexity, we usually focus on the number of subsets and ignore the size of each subset, so the space complexity is O(2^N).

Python.code:
def find_all_subsets(input_set):
    all_subsets = [[]] 

    for current_number in input_set:
        # Iterate through each number in the input set

        new_subsets = []

        for existing_subset in all_subsets:
            # Create new subsets including current number
            new_subsets.append(existing_subset + [current_number])

        # Add the new subsets to the collection
        all_subsets.extend(new_subsets)

    return all_subsets

JavaScript.html:
function findSubsets(numbers) {
  const allSubsets = [[]];

  for (const number of numbers) {
    const numberOfExistingSubsets = allSubsets.length;

    // We iterate through existing subsets to avoid mutation issues

    for (let i = 0; i < numberOfExistingSubsets; i++) {
      const existingSubset = allSubsets[i];

      // Create a new subset by adding the current number

      const newSubset = [...existingSubset, number];

      // Add the new subset to the list of all subsets
      allSubsets.push(newSubset);
    }
  }

  return allSubsets;
}

      // Iterate through the existing subsets
      for (let i = 0; i < numberOfExistingSubsets; i++) {

        // Create a new subset by adding current number
        const existingSubset = allSubsets[i];
        const newSubset = [...existingSubset, number];

        // Add the new subset to the list
        allSubsets.push(newSubset);
      }
    }

    return allSubsets;
  }

Java.html:
public class Solution {

    public java.util.List<java.util.List<Integer>> subsets(int[] numbers) {
        java.util.List<java.util.List<Integer>> allSubsets = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
        // Initially, the only subset is the empty set.
        allSubsets.add(new java.util.ArrayList<>());

        for (int number : numbers) {
            int numberOfExistingSubsets = allSubsets.size();

            // Iterate through existing subsets.
            for (int i = 0; i < numberOfExistingSubsets; i++) {
                java.util.List<Integer> currentSubset = new java.util.ArrayList<>(allSubsets.get(i));

                // Create a new subset by adding the current number.
                currentSubset.add(number);

                // Add the new subset to the list of all subsets.
                // By adding, we consider the 'include' case.
                allSubsets.add(currentSubset);

            }

        }

        return allSubsets;

    }

}

C++.html:
#include <vector>

std::vector<std::vector<int>> findSubsets(std::vector<int>& nums) {
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> allSubsets;
    allSubsets.push_back({});

    for (int currentNumber : nums) {
        // Iterate through numbers
        int numberOfExistingSubsets = allSubsets.size();

        // Iterate through existing subsets
        for (int i = 0; i < numberOfExistingSubsets; ++i) {
            // Create new subsets with current number included
            std::vector<int> subsetWithCurrentNumber = allSubsets[i];
            subsetWithCurrentNumber.push_back(currentNumber);
            allSubsets.push_back(subsetWithCurrentNumber);
        }

        // Each number doubles the total subsets
    }

    return allSubsets;
}

C#.html:
using System.Collections.Generic;

public class Solution {
    public IList<IList<int>> Subsets(int[] numbers) {
        IList<IList<int>> allSubsets = new List<IList<int>>();
        // Start with an empty set
        allSubsets.Add(new List<int>());

        foreach (int number in numbers) {
            int numberOfExistingSubsets = allSubsets.Count;

            // Iterate through existing subsets to extend them
            for (int i = 0; i < numberOfExistingSubsets; i++) {
                // Create a copy of the existing subset
                List<int> subsetCopy = new List<int>(allSubsets[i]);
                // Add the current number to the copy
                subsetCopy.Add(number);

                // Adding a copy to avoid modifying the original
                allSubsets.Add(subsetCopy);
            }
        }

        return allSubsets;
    }
}

Go.html:
package main

func subsets(nums []int) [][]int {
	subsetsList := [][]int{{}}

	// Iterate through each number
	for _, num := range nums {
		currentSubsetsLength := len(subsetsList)

		// Iterate through existing subsets
		for i := 0; i < currentSubsetsLength; i++ {
			// Create a new subset by adding the current number
			newSubset := make([]int, len(subsetsList[i]))
			copy(newSubset, subsetsList[i])
			newSubset = append(newSubset, num)

			// Append the new subset to the list
			subsetsList = append(subsetsList, newSubset)
		}
	}

	return subsetsList
}

Rust.html:
fn subsets(numbers: Vec<i32>) -> Vec<Vec<i32>> {
    let mut all_subsets: Vec<Vec<i32>> = Vec::new();
    all_subsets.push(Vec::new());

    for &number in &numbers {
      // We iterate through each number
      let mut new_subsets: Vec<Vec<i32>> = Vec::new();

      for existing_subset in &all_subsets {
        // Copy the current subsets with the number added.
        let mut subset_with_number = existing_subset.clone();
        subset_with_number.push(number);
        new_subsets.push(subset_with_number);

        // Include existing subsets to account for exclusion.
        new_subsets.push(existing_subset.clone());

Kotlin.html:
fun findSubsets(inputNumbers: IntArray): List<List<Int>> {
    val allSubsets: MutableList<MutableList<Int>> = mutableListOf()
    allSubsets.add(mutableListOf()) // Start with the empty set.

    for (number in inputNumbers) {
      val numberOfSubsets = allSubsets.size

      // Iterate through existing subsets
      for (i in 0 until numberOfSubsets) {
        val currentSubset = allSubsets[i].toMutableList()

        // Create a new subset by adding the current number.
        currentSubset.add(number)
        allSubsets.add(currentSubset)
      }
    }

    return allSubsets
  }

Swift.html:
struct SubsetsSolver {
    func findSubsets(numbers: [Int]) -> [[Int]] {
        var allSubsets: [[Int]] = [[]]

        for currentNumber in numbers {

            // Iterate through all existing subsets
            var newSubsets: [[Int]] = []

            for existingSubset in allSubsets {

                // Create a new subset including current number
                var newSubset = existingSubset
                newSubset.append(currentNumber)
                newSubsets.append(newSubset)
            }

            // Add the new subsets to the collection
            allSubsets.append(contentsOf: newSubsets)
        }

        return allSubsets
    }
}

struct SubsetsSolverRecursive {
    func findSubsetsRecursive(numbers: [Int]) -> [[Int]] {
        var allSubsets: [[Int]] = []
        var currentSubset: [Int] = []
        
        findSubsetsHelper(numbers: numbers, index: 0, currentSubset: &currentSubset, allSubsets: &allSubsets)
        
        return allSubsets
    }

    private func findSubsetsHelper(numbers: [Int], index: Int, currentSubset: inout [Int], allSubsets: inout [[Int]]) {

        // Base Case - add current subset to all
        if index == numbers.count {
            allSubsets.append(currentSubset)
            return
        }

        // Exclude the current number
        findSubsetsHelper(numbers: numbers, index: index + 1, currentSubset: &currentSubset, allSubsets: &allSubsets)

        // Include the current number in the subset
        currentSubset.append(numbers[index])

        // Explore further with the inclusion
        findSubsetsHelper(numbers: numbers, index: index + 1, currentSubset: &currentSubset, allSubsets: &allSubsets)

        // Backtrack: Remove the current number
        currentSubset.removeLast()
    }
}

struct SubsetsSolverIterative2 {
    func findSubsetsIterative(numbers: [Int]) -> [[Int]] {
        var allSubsets: [[Int]] = [[]]

        for number in numbers {
            let currentSubsetsCount = allSubsets.count

            // Iterate through all the existing subsets
            for i in 0..<currentSubsetsCount {
                // Create a new subset based on each existing subset
                var newSubset = allSubsets[i]
                newSubset.append(number)

                // Add the new subset to the list of all subsets
                allSubsets.append(newSubset)
            }
        }

        return allSubsets
    }
}

Ruby.code:
def find_all_subsets(original_collection)
  all_subsets = [[]]

  original_collection.each do |item_from_collection|
    # Iterate through the collection and build subsets
    new_subsets = all_subsets.map do |existing_subset|
      existing_subset + [item_from_collection]
    end

    # Add the new subsets to the total list
    all_subsets.concat(new_subsets)
  end

  # Returning all of the subsets
  return all_subsets
end

    end

    # Add the new subsets to the total list
    all_subsets.concat(new_subsets)
  end

  # Returning all of the subsets
  return all_subsets
end

PHP.code:
<?php
function findSubsets(array $inputNumbers): array
{
    $allSubsets = [[]];

    foreach ($inputNumbers as $currentNumber) {
        $newSubsets = [];

        // Iterate over existing subsets to extend them.
        foreach ($allSubsets as $existingSubset) {
            // Add the existing subset as is.
            $newSubsets[] = $existingSubset;

            // Create a new subset with the current number.
            $subsetWithCurrentNumber = array_merge($existingSubset, [$currentNumber]);
            $newSubsets[] = $subsetWithCurrentNumber;
        }

        // Update the master list with the new subsets.
        $allSubsets = $newSubsets;
    }

    return $allSubsets;
}

# Example Usage:
# $numbers = [1, 2, 3];
# $subsets = findSubsets($numbers);
# print_r($subsets);

?>

function findSubsets(array $inputNumbers): array
{
    $allSubsets = [[]];

    foreach ($inputNumbers as $currentNumber) {
        $newSubsets = [];

        // Iterate over existing subsets to extend them.
        foreach ($allSubsets as $existingSubset) {
            // Add the existing subset as is.
            $newSubsets[] = $existingSubset;

            // Create a new subset with the current number.
            $subsetWithCurrentNumber = array_merge($existingSubset, [$currentNumber]);
            $newSubsets[] = $subsetWithCurrentNumber;
        }

        // Update the master list with the new subsets.
        $allSubsets = $newSubsets;
    }
